message:
refactor(resolv): harden dns_message_slice length math + idiomatic byte check

Two small cleanups in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`:

1. `dns_message_slice` previously computed
       let len = unsafe { eom.offset_from(msg) };
   then cast through `as usize`. `pointer::offset_from` panics in
   debug builds when both pointers are not in the same allocation
   and is otherwise UB on disjoint pointers, so it's the wrong tool
   for "are these two raw pointers far apart enough to enclose a DNS
   message". We've already established `msg <= eom` immediately
   above, so we can convert each pointer to `usize` and use
   `checked_sub` to compute the length without invoking
   `offset_from`. We also tighten the upper bound: once we know the
   subtraction succeeded, reject any `len > isize::MAX as usize`,
   which preserves the invariant required by
   `core::slice::from_raw_parts` while removing the implicit
   `isize -> usize` round-trip.

2. `parse_signed_meters` had a `!bytes.is_empty() && *bytes.last().unwrap() == b'm'`
   guard for the optional trailing `'m'`. Replace with the more
   idiomatic `bytes.last().copied() == Some(b'm')`, eliminating the
   `unwrap()` and the empty-check.

Behavior is unchanged for any well-formed input; only the failure
mode for pathological pointer pairs is tightened (return `None`
instead of relying on `offset_from`'s preconditions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
